heading: Enforcement
label: 4910 Enforcement
notes: Amendments1978—Subsec. (a). redesignated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. L. 95–609
amendments: Amendments1978—Subsec. (a). redesignated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. L. 95–609

children.text: Any person who willfully or knowingly violates paragraph (1), (3), (5), or (6) of subsection (a) of shall be punished by a fine of not more than $25,000 per day of violation, or by imprisonment for not more than one year, or by both. If the conviction is for a violation committed after a first conviction of such person under this subsection, punishment shall be by a fine of not more than $50,000 per day of violation, or by imprisonment for not more than two years, or by both.section 4909 of this title
Any person who violates paragraph (1), (3), (5), or (6) of subsection (a) of shall be subject to a civil penalty not to exceed $10,000 per day of such violation.section 4909 of this title

children.heading: Separate violations
children.label: b Separate violations
children.text: For the purpose of this section, each day of violation of any paragraph of shall constitute a separate violation of that section.section 4909(a) of this title

children.heading: Actions to restrain violations
children.label: c Actions to restrain violations
children.text: The district courts of the United States shall have jurisdiction of actions brought by and in the name of the United States to restrain any violations of .section 4909(a) of this title

children.heading: Orders issued to protect public health and welfare; notice; opportunity for hearing
children.label: d Orders issued to protect public health and welfare; notice; opportunity for hearing

children.text: Whenever any person is in violation of , the Administrator may issue an order specifying such relief as he determines is necessary to protect the public health and welfare.section 4909(a) of this title
Any order under this subsection shall be issued only after notice and opportunity for a hearing in accordance with .section 554 of title 5

children.heading: “Person” defined
children.label: e “Person” defined
children.text: The term “person,” as used in this section, does not include a department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States.
